title: caryophyllus atrorubens
intro: Caryophyllus atrorubens, also known as the sweet clover, is a perennial plant belonging to the carnation family. It is known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of coumarin, a natural anticoagulant. The health benefits of caryophyllus atrorubens include its ability to prevent blood clots, reduce inflammation, and aid in the treatment of various cardiovascular diseases. Herbal preparations of caryophyllus atrorubens are often made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support heart health and prevent the formation of blood clots.
